_synthesis_note: SYNTHESIZED quantitative signal for the Stripe Connect onboarding worked example. Numbers are illustrative, not measured. Each step count and failure signal is grounded in a public-signal friction pattern documented in PHASE_2_RESEARCH.md; the per-number rationale is in PHASE_2_NOTES.md. Shape mirrors funnel-researcher/examples/api_activation/dropoff_data.json (the schema the tool consumes).

target_step_failure_signals.signal: Account.requirements.currently_due remains non-empty 7+ days after the last POST /v1/accounts/{account} submit; no further API calls from the integration (silent abandonment)
target_step_failure_signals.grounded_in: P1 — the silent requirements.currently_due gate (PHASE_2_RESEARCH.md C: Stripe Support KB 'payouts-or-charges-not-enabled'; D: future_requirements changelog; A: charges_enabled has zero stripe-python issues)

target_step_failure_signals.signal: Requested capability stays status=inactive/pending with capability.requirements.currently_due populated; developer keeps polling GET /v1/accounts/{account}/capabilities/{capability} without submitting the new requirements
target_step_failure_signals.grounded_in: P2 — capabilities<->requirements coupling (changelog 'risk requirements added to Capabilities API'; account-capabilities doc; Support KB)

target_step_failure_signals.signal: Account.requirements.disabled_reason == 'requirements.pending_verification'; integration stops listening for account.updated and never re-checks
target_step_failure_signals.grounded_in: P1 — async verification stall (handling-api-verification.md disabled_reason table; errors.md Layer 3a)

target_step_failure_signals.signal: POST /v1/accounts/{account} returns 200 but Account.requirements.currently_due is unchanged (submitted under-specified or wrong fields, no error raised)
target_step_failure_signals.grounded_in: P4 + P1 — 200 without progress, error-to-next-action gap (GitHub #347; errors.md Layer 2)

target_step_failure_signals.signal: Account.requirements.errors[] populated (e.g. invalid_address_city_state_postal_code, verification_document_failed_greyscale) and not resolved before the cohort window closes
target_step_failure_signals.grounded_in: P4 — validation/verification error codes not surfaced as next actions (handling-api-verification.md requirements.errors[]; errors.md Layer 3b)

qualitative_signals: stripe-python issue #1227 ('AttributeError: service_agreement', open, 20 comments): typed Account.tos_acceptance.service_agreement access crashes onboarding gating logic the type hints implied was safe — interface-layer friction adjacent to the requirements gate.

Stripe maintains dedicated Support KB articles for 'payouts or charges not enabled for connected accounts with platform-gathered verification requirements' and 'manage onboarding and risk requirements' — the friction is common enough to warrant standing documentation.

Stack Overflow's highest-signal stripe-connect onboarding questions are conceptual ('what's the difference between Customers and Accounts?'), not code bugs — the gate is upstream of code (context/measurement), consistent with charges_enabled producing zero stripe-python SDK issues.

Stripe changelog shows repeated breaking changes to the requirements/capabilities contract (future_requirements added, risk requirements added to the Capabilities API, requirements-collection parameters updated) — Stripe iterating on exactly the surface where this funnel's dropoff concentrates.
